notes:
Opening turn notes. Inspected current opportunity, get_case, list_evidence, stat_evidence, read_evidence_range. Visible record contains only ev_e61df84283cc_market-page, market-page.txt, sha256 e61df84283cc858bfd0cf8235c6f1d53a10d6e1c37b2d97256da8f1e6391cc17, 4971 bytes, case packet. Packet establishes the proposition, market metadata, capture time 2026-05-24T01:44:04Z, and market rule text. Decisive elements: Iran initiated; non-weather; by May 24 2026 11:59 PM ET; major closure via broad closure/cancellation/suspension of commercial flights through/into/out of Iranian airspace or major Iranian airspace region, or complete suspension of arrivals/departures at at least two listed airports. Packet says primary resolution sources are official Iranian aviation authorities and consensus credible reporting; warnings/no-fly zones by others do not suffice.

Source map and investigation: searched web for Iran airspace May 24 2026, Reuters, NOTAM May 24 Tehran, OIIX A1010/26, western Tehran FIR May 25 NOTAM. Leads: Notamify page for OIIX A1010/26 appears to preserve raw NOTAM text with B)2605222046 C)2605250830EST, all airports in west part of Tehran FIR closed except OITR/OIKK/OIAA/OISS/OIYY/OICC/OIGG/OIAW, sunrise-to-sunset operations, previous permissions suspended, new CAA permission required for civil IFR passenger flights. Secondary/corroborating leads include Indian Express, Arab Times, Siasat, Kenya Times reporting Iran CAA-issued NOTAM closing western Tehran FIR airports until May 25 amid security tensions; SafeAirspace/OPSGROUP/EASA context on Iranian airspace restrictions and west Tehran FIR remaining closed. Adverse lead: social snippet claims no new official/accepted NOTAM or says only western flight suspension remained in place; needs capture/evaluation if raised. Also exceptions/daylight approvals mean the best plaintiff theory is broad closure of a major Iranian airspace region, not necessarily the alternative complete-suspension-at-two-listed-airports prong.

Opening strategy: Because openings cannot submit evidence and outside sources are not admitted, do not claim the NOTAM contents as record proof. Use ev_market-page for the rule framework and preview that plaintiff will seek admission of raw NOTAM/official aviation material and corroborating credible reporting. Acknowledge missing link and provenance issues: the record currently proves the rule, not the actual closure. Later argument should admit source materials before relying on them.

Defendant opening work notes, 2026-06-19. Opportunity openings:defendant, final action record_opening_statement only; evidence submission barred. Inspected get_case/list_evidence/stat/read. Current record has one admitted case-packet item ev_e61df84283cc_market-page (sha256 e61df84283cc858bfd0cf8235c6f1d53a10d6e1c37b2d97256da8f1e6391cc17, 4971 bytes, text/plain) and plaintiff opening. Full packet read. Packet proves rule, deadline, operative elements, examples, and source hierarchy; it does not prove any May 2026 factual closure event. Rule requires Iran-initiated non-weather major closure by May 24 11:59 PM ET; major closure must be broad closure/cancellation/suspension of commercial flights transiting, arriving in, and departing from Iranian airspace or a major Iranian airspace region; limited cancellations/delays/partial closures do not qualify; limited exceptions do not defeat a broad closure; separate airport prong requires complete non-weather suspension of commercial arrivals and departures affecting at least two listed airports (IKA, THR, MHD, SYZ, IFN). Foreign warnings/reroutes insufficient. Primary sources are official Iranian aviation authority info and consensus credible reporting.

Native searches before opening: searched web for May 24/May 22 2026 Iran airspace closure, Tehran FIR, NOTAM, OIIX A1010/26, western Tehran FIR. Leads include SafeAirspace, Indian Express, AbdiMedia, Kenya Times, Siasat, DefconLevel, social posts/snippets. Leads suggest reported NOTAM OIIX A1010/26 effective May 22-May 25 affecting western Tehran FIR, closure of airports in western part, cancellation of prior permissions, fresh CAA approval for IFR passenger flights, daylight-only limited operations at named airports. Important ambiguity: some leads describe airport closures/restrictions rather than a broad airspace closure; some snippets mention a later/contrary official statement that no new NOTAM was issued and western suspension remained in place. No outside source is admitted, so opening should not rely on them as proof.

Defense theory: plaintiff has burden. Current admitted evidence does not establish the event. The case will turn on raw official NOTAM/provenance, whether it was Iran-issued, timing before 2026-05-25 03:59 UTC, non-weather reason, and scope. Defense should focus on market-rule fit: reported partial airport restrictions, daylight operations, permission requirements, or foreign airline reroutes may not equal a qualifying major closure of commercial flights transiting/arriving/departing through Iranian airspace or a major region. Airport prong may fail if at least two listed airports did not have complete suspension of commercial arrivals/departures. Evidence that would change position: official raw NOTAM or credible consensus showing ordinary commercial operations through a major Iranian airspace region were broadly stopped by Iran before the deadline with only narrow exceptions. Journal updated at /aar-codex/aar-work-notes.md with source map, searches, and unresolved gaps.

Merits turn notes. Wait returned ready for arguments:plaintiff. Refreshed get_case/list_evidence/stat_evidence; visible record initially only market-page plus openings. Defendant opening framed expected adverse points: packet proves rule only; a reported NOTAM may be partial airport-operation restriction; permission/daylight exceptions may not be broad closure; need source-chain quality.

Investigation/capture: Opened Notamify OIIX A1010/26 page, Indian Express May 23 article, Arab Times May 23 article, SafeAirspace Iran page, and EASA Iran advisory page. Submitted four source captures as plaintiff evidence: ev_c909c4e71960... Notamify raw NOTAM; ev_a701f560aa64... Indian Express corroborating report; ev_60a74d32aeaf... Arab Times corroborating report; ev_0beb299b6e6b... SafeAirspace context. EASA page was official EU risk context but older/withdrawn 2026-02-R1 and not submitted because it does not directly prove May 24 closure and could distract.

Evidence analysis: Notamify core raw NOTAM shows A1010/26 NOTAMR A0799/26, OIIX, B)2605222046 C)2605250830EST, all airports in west part of Tehran FIR closed except OITR/OIKK/OIAA/OISS/OIYY/OICC/OIGG/OIAW, which operate sunrise to sunset; all previous permissions suspended; new CAA permission required for civil IFR passenger flights. This proves scope/timing if credited, but provenance is third-party database not official Iranian page. Indian Express and Arab Times corroborate Iran CAA issuance, May 22-May 25 timing, limited exceptions, fresh approval, and non-weather security/military context. SafeAirspace gives context that western Tehran FIR remained closed in June and shows significance of west OIIX restrictions; not primary proof.

Theory: Use broad major-region prong, not alternate two-listed-airport complete-suspension prong. Rule covers broad closure/cancellation/suspension of commercial flights transiting/arriving/departing from major Iranian airspace region, and limited exceptions do not disqualify broad closure. Iran initiation shown by CAA/NOTAM and new CAA permission. Timing includes May 24 11:59 PM ET. Non-weather shown by security/tensions context and absence of weather explanation. Major shown by all west Tehran FIR airports closed except limited daylight airports and all prior permissions suspended.

Adverse/gaps: Official Iran CAA page not captured. Social snippets suggested 'no new NOTAM issued' / no official accepted NOTAM, but not followed to reliable source within time and possibly later/ambiguous. If rebuttal opportunity arises, capture exact adverse source if defendant relies on it. Notamify inactive status is expected because retrieval occurred after the May 22-25 period. Journal updated at /aar-codex/aar-work-notes.md.

Defendant merits argument work notes. Opportunity arguments:defendant. Record checked via get_case/list_evidence/stat/read. Plaintiff submitted four evidence items. Read full submitted evidence bytes for ev_c909c4e71960 (Notamify A1010/26), ev_a701f560aa64 (Indian Express), ev_60a74d32aeaf (Arab Times), ev_0beb299b6e6b (SafeAirspace). Core plaintiff exhibit itself labels A1010/26 as AD Airport Operations; raw Q line: Q)OIIX/QFALC/IV/NBO/A/000/999/; E text closes all airports in west part of Tehran FIR except eight listed airports operational sunrise-sunset, suspends previous permissions, and requires new CAA permission for civil IFR passenger flights on these airports. Indian Express uses broad airspace language but says NOTAM ordered shutdown of all airports in western Tehran FIR and attributes NOTAM fact to Gulf News; also says Tehran Mehrabad, Imam Khomeini, Isfahan, Shiraz, Mashhad remain operational with daylight/fresh approval. Arab Times likewise airport-focused; says fresh approval before civil IFR passenger flights to or over affected airports. SafeAirspace is June context, not May raw NOTAM.

Defense investigation: searched QFALC/QAFLC code meanings. Found FAA Order 7930.2 Appendix B. It says NOTAM Q code has five letters; second/third identify subject; fourth/fifth identify status; AF = Flight information region; FA = Aerodrome; LC = Closed. Submitted as ev_d104b8c2ce1a. Opened Notamify A0225/26 January 2026 Tehran FIR closure comparison (market packet qualifying example): page labels ASP Airspace Restrictions; raw Q) OIIX/QAFLC/IV/NBO/E/000/999/; E text says TEHRAN FIR CLSD TO ALL FLIGHTS except approved international civil arrivals/departures. Submitted as ev_089fa1cded7e.

Argument plan: concede timing and non-weather/security are likely shown. Contest major closure. Plaintiff has not carried burden because the raw A1010/26 is an aerodrome-closure NOTAM, not a flight-information-region/airspace closure. It does not say western Tehran FIR airspace closed to all flights or that commercial overflights/transiting were suspended generally. Secondary reports cannot broaden the raw NOTAM, especially where they also describe airports. Airport prong fails because plaintiff's own evidence says IKA, THR, MHD, SYZ, IFN remained operational under restrictions, not complete suspension. Limited-exception clause only saves a broad closure; it does not convert an airport-specific partial regime into a major airspace closure. Source-chain gap: no official Iranian source and no A0796/A0799 capture; plaintiff relies on third-party database plus summaries. Journal updated at /aar-codex/aar-work-notes.md.

Rebuttal notes. Rebuttal opportunity ready. Scanned evidence list and stat/read defendant evidence. Defendant submitted ev_d104b8c2ce1a FAA Q-code reference and ev_089fa1cded7e Notamify January 2026 comparison. Exact reads confirm FAA source: FA=aerodrome, AF=flight information region, LC=closed. Defendant's January comparison uses QAFLC and says Tehran FIR closed to all flights except international civil arrivals/departures with prior Iran CAA permission. Plaintiff A1010/26 uses QFALC and airport operations language. Rebuttal strategy: do not deny Q-code distinction. Argue it is not dispositive because market rule covers broad closure/cancellation/suspension of commercial flights transiting, arriving in, and departing from Iranian airspace or a major Iranian airspace region. A western Tehran FIR aerodrome closure that shuts all west-region airports except daylight listed airports, suspends all prior permissions, and requires new CAA approval for civil IFR passenger flights is a broad suspension of commercial arrivals/departures in a major Iranian airspace region. Limited CAA-approved exceptions are expressly permitted by the rule. January example being broader does not make May restriction non-qualifying; the market packet's text controls and also references April western airspace closure as qualifying. Avoid alternate two-listed-airport complete-suspension prong.

Defendant surrebuttal notes. Opportunity surrebuttals:defendant. Inspected get_case/list_evidence. No new plaintiff evidence was submitted in rebuttal; plaintiff offered/reused existing FAA Q-code, January comparison, and A1010/26 exhibits and filed a rebuttal technical report. Plaintiff rebuttal concedes the code point: A1010/26 is QFALC/aerodrome closed while January comparator is QAFLC/FIR closed. Plaintiff's response is interpretive: market rule includes arriving/departing, is not limited to AF-coded full FIR closures, and limited CAA-approved exceptions fit A1010/26. No new outside research needed because rebuttal raised no new factual source requiring capture. Surrebuttal plan: focus on conceded raw-notice distinction; the rule requires a broad closure/suspension of commercial flights transiting, arriving in, and departing from airspace/major region, while plaintiff drops overflight/transit and relies on an airport-coded notice. Limited exceptions can preserve a broad closure but cannot create one from a partial airport regime with eight operational airports. Defense does not claim only whole-FIR AF code can ever qualify; it claims plaintiff must prove equivalent broad commercial-flight suspension, which the raw A1010/26 text does not. Airport prong remains unavailable because plaintiff does not rely on it and its evidence says key listed airports were operational under restrictions. Journal updated at /aar-codex/aar-work-notes.md.

Closing notes. Closing opportunity ready. Refreshed get_case/list_evidence/stat for core plaintiff NOTAM and defendant FAA code evidence. Current record includes plaintiff argument/rebuttal, defendant argument/surrebuttal, plaintiff exhibits PX-1..PX-5, defendant Q-code and January comparator evidence, and technical reports. No new case-packet evidence. Defendant's strongest final point: A1010/26 is QFALC/aerodrome closure and January comparator QAFLC/FIR closure; raw text does not bar overflights and says airports. Plaintiff closing should acknowledge this and focus on rule text: broad closure/cancellation/suspension of commercial flights transiting, arriving in, and departing from Iranian airspace or a major Iranian airspace region, plus limited exceptions clause. The admitted record shows Iran CAA-linked NOTAM A1010/26 effective May 22 20:46 UTC to May 25 08:30 UTC, west part of Tehran FIR, all airports closed except eight daylight-limited airports, all previous permissions suspended, fresh CAA permission required for civil IFR passenger flights; corroborating reports support CAA issuance, western sector/airspace closure, security/non-weather context. Weaknesses remain: Notamify is third-party, official Iran CAA page not captured, and alternate listed-airport complete-suspension prong is weak because major airports remained operational under restrictions. Closing should not rely on alternate prong or overclaim full overflight closure.

Defendant closing notes. Opportunity closings:defendant. Inspected current record and list_evidence. Plaintiff closing filed; no new evidence added. Closing phase bars new evidence and technical reports. Exact evidence contents were read earlier and remain decisive: market rule; A1010/26 raw Notamify capture; FAA Q-code table; January Notamify comparator; secondary reports. Plan: concede plaintiff's strongest points (Iran CAA-linked notice, timely, non-weather/security context, serious restrictions) but stress burden on market-defined major closure. Best evidence is raw A1010/26: AD Airport Operations, QFALC/aerodrome closed, all airports in west Tehran FIR closed except eight, fresh permission for civil IFR passenger flights on these airports. Compare January qualifying example: ASP Airspace Restrictions, QAFLC/FIR closed, E text says Tehran FIR closed to all flights. Rule excludes partial closures and limited restrictions; limited exceptions only save a closure after plaintiff proves it is broad. Airport prong fails because plaintiff's own evidence says listed airports remained operational. Source/provenance gaps remain: no official Iranian page, no referenced A0796/A0799 source, secondary reports partly derivative. Journal updated at /aar-codex/aar-work-notes.md.
